title: IPv4 Addressing, CIDR, VLSM, and Subnet Design · Incident Case: Net Ipv4 Address Plan
summary: Create an address plan for departments, infrastructure, guests, point-to-point links, growth, and route summarization. Planned artifact: Add and verify the net ipv4 address plan increment in a validated IPv4 subnet allocation workbook. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Document scopes, gateways, reservations, exclusions, VLANs, DHCP pools, static infrastructure, and IPAM ownership.
Recognize class A through E historical boundaries without using classful thinking to replace CIDR design.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# comptia-network-plus safe operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic browser simulator.
# Add one observation, command, expected signal, and conclusion per activity.

steps.title: Predict Net Ipv4 Address Plan
steps.instruction:
Use a changed theory case derived from this module: network ipv4 subnetting. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for document scopes, gateways, reservations, exclusions, vlans, dhcp pools, static infrastructure, and ipam ownership.

content.text: Document scopes, gateways, reservations, exclusions, VLANs, DHCP pools, static infrastructure, and IPAM ownership. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.text: An address spreadsheet is complete even when it omits VLAN and DHCP relationships. This fails the Net Ipv4 Address Plan contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.
